betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Fission Fuels§f:
Fission Fuels are processed fuel forms ready to be loaded into a Solid Fission Reactor. Using the Isotope Separator's enriched outputs, you fabricate the various pellet and fuel-rod forms that the reactor consumes.

§6Standard Pellet Fuels (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:417-427)§f:
NC defines eight element families of fission fuel. Each is crafted via the fissionFuelRecipes() helper:
- Uranium: pellet_uranium + fuel_uranium (fertile U-238, fissile U-233, U-235)
- Neptunium: pellet_neptunium + fuel_neptunium (Np-237, Np-236)
- Plutonium: pellet_plutonium + fuel_plutonium (Pu-242, Pu-239, Pu-241)
- Mixed (MIX-239, MIX-241): pellet_mixed + fuel_mixed (U-238 + Pu-239 or Pu-241)
- Americium: pellet_americium + fuel_americium (Am-243, Am-242)
- Curium: pellet_curium + fuel_curium (Cm-246, Cm-243/245/247)
- Berkelium: pellet_berkelium + fuel_berkelium (Bk-247, Bk-248)
- Californium: pellet_californium + fuel_californium (Cf-252, Cf-249/251)

§6LE vs HE Forms (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:575-578)§f:
For each fissile isotope, two enrichment grades are made:
- Low-Enriched (LE): 8 fertile + 1 fissile -> ingotLE{Fuel}{fissile}{type}, lower power, longer lifetime
- High-Enriched (HE): 6 fertile + 3 fissile -> ingotHE{Fuel}{fissile}{type}, higher power, higher heat

§6Fuel Forms (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:596)§f:
Each fuel is fabricated in four cladding forms:
- plain (default): bare metal fuel
- Carbide: fuel + dustGraphite (from Separator)
- Oxide: fuel + dustOxygen (implicit in process)
- Nitride: fuel + dustNitrogen (implicit in process)
- ZA (Zirconium Alloy): fuel + dustZirconium (from Separator)

§6Pellets vs Fuel Rods (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:575-578)§f:
- Pellets: meta 0/4, single-form (plain or Carbide). Direct fuel item.
- Fuel rods: meta 8/12/16/20, three forms (Oxide, Nitride, ZA). Higher capacity, requires insertion into a fuel-rod assembly before reactor loading.

§6Mixed Oxide (MOX) Fuels (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:420-423)§f:
MIX-239 and MIX-241 are special: they blend Uranium-238 with Plutonium-239 or 241. They are produced as both pellets (plain/Carbide) and fuel rods (Oxide/Nitride/ZA). MOX fuels are higher power than pure-uranium fuels but require a more advanced reactor cooling system.

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The quest's task item is nuclearcraft:pellet_uranium. Other graph descendants include all the pellet_mixed, pellet_americium, pellet_curium, pellet_berkelium, pellet_californium, and pellet_neptunium forms. These are the inputs to the Solid Fission Reactor multiblock.

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires the Isotope Separator (q30041) to be unlocked. Without enriched U-235, Pu-239, or other fissile streams, you cannot craft any fission fuel.

§eTip§f: Start with LEU-235 pellets — they have the longest lifetime (4800t) and lowest heat (Heat 120, Efficiency 1.0), making them the safest introduction to fission.

§eTip§f: When scaling up, switch to HEU-235 or MOX fuels for higher power density. Pair them with a dedicated water/coolant loop and a heat-exchange turbine to extract useful work.

§cWarning§f: All fission fuels are intensely radioactive. Wear a full hazmat suit with a respirator, and never store fuel rods in a wooden chest — they will irradiate the surrounding area.
